included.description: Full-service TA travel center at I-40 Exit 37 (Wiggins Rd), open 24/7. 106 truck parking spots (38 reservable), 8 diesel fuel lanes, 8 showers, restaurant/deli, convenience store, laundry, CAT scale, bulk DEF, and TA Truck Service shop with 4 bays offering tire repair, oil/lube, DOT inspections and truck repairs (truck service: 828-641-9658). Confirmed operating post-Hurricane Helene (reviews through mid-2026).

Certified CAT scale located at the TA Candler travel center, I-40 Exit 37, open 24 hours with the truck stop. The only verified CAT scale on I-40 between Canton and Old Fort in the Asheville metro segment.

Independent heavy-duty truck and trailer repair shop at I-40 Exit 37 in Candler, serving western NC since 2016. Fixed shop location (not mobile-only) offering diesel engine repair, commercial truck tire installation and repair, hydraulic hose repair, and RV/heavy equipment work. First-come, first-served; open weekdays with extended evening hours, closed weekends. Operating post-Hurricane Helene (reviews through 2026).

Freightliner dealership with fixed full-service truck repair, parts and maintenance facility in the Enka-Candler commerce area off Smokey Park Hwy (US 19/23), near I-40 Exit 44. Parts and service departments open 7 days a week; handles routine maintenance through major semi-truck repairs plus new/used truck sales.

84-room motel just off I-40 Exit 65 (US 70), two blocks from downtown Black Mountain. Explicitly advertises plenty of free lot parking for trucks and RVs and is listed on Trucker Path as a truck-friendly hotel with RV/bus/truck parking. Free breakfast, free WiFi, laundry facilities, 24-hour front desk. Operating post-Hurricane Helene (2026 reviews/rates active).

57-room Choice Hotels property just off I-40 Exit 64 (NC 9) in Black Mountain. Multiple booking listings explicitly note on-site RV, bus, and truck parking. Free hot breakfast, free WiFi, seasonal outdoor pool, guest laundry. Operating post-Hurricane Helene (2026 reviews/rates active).

held.business_name: Asheville Weigh Station (NC State Highway Patrol)
held.address: I-40 eastbound and westbound, approx. 12 miles west of Asheville (Buncombe County)
held.description: NCSHP-operated weigh station with two fixed scales on I-40 in Buncombe County (between exits 37 and 44, near Candler). HELD: official NCSHP/NCDPS listings confirm the station and phone number but publish no civic street address; the '410 I-40, Candler' / 'Mile Marker 41' addresses circulating online appear only on unofficial directories.
held.held_reason: Official NC source (NCSHP/NCDPS) confirms station but provides no civic address; rule requires official source WITH civic address.

held.business_name: Truck Parking Club - 1464 Brevard Rd lot
held.description: Bookable truck/trailer parking lot on Brevard Rd (NC 191) listed on Truck Parking Club: approx. 14-15 spaces, $15/day, $85/week, $250/month, 5.0-star member rating. HELD: single-source (Truck Parking Club marketplace only); 1464 Brevard Rd sits on the NC 191 corridor between I-40 Exit 47 and I-26 Exit 33, so I-40 exit assignment is unverified.
held.held_reason: Single source (Truck Parking Club listing only); exit proximity (I-40 Exit 47 vs I-26 Exit 33) unverified.

held.business_name: Truck Parking Club - Candler lot off Smokey Park Hwy
held.address: Smokey Park Hwy (exact street number not published in listing preview)
held.description: Bookable truck/trailer parking off Smokey Park Hwy (US 19/23) in Candler listed on Truck Parking Club: $17/day, $75/week, $200/month, no low-boys, 5.0-star member rating; a second Candler listing shows 10 spaces at $15/day. HELD: single-source (Truck Parking Club marketplace only) and full street address not exposed without booking; Smokey Park Hwy corridor is served by I-40 exits 37 and 44.
held.held_reason: Single source (Truck Parking Club listing only); exact civic address not published in listing preview.

held.business_name: Comfort Inn Asheville East-Blue Ridge Pkwy Access
held.description: Choice Hotels property on Tunnel Rd (US 70) at I-40 Exit 55 in east Asheville. Trucker-reported parking arrangement: large vehicles, tractor-trailers and trailers park across the street in the vacant lot east of the Waffle House, per crowd-sourced HotelTruckParking reports. HELD: truck-parking confirmation is a single crowd-sourced report describing off-site (not hotel-controlled) parking, and listed phone numbers conflict across sources.
held.held_reason: Truck-parking confirmation from a single crowd-sourced source and describes an off-site vacant lot; phone number unverified (conflicting listings).

held.description: Tire dealer and service center in West Asheville advertising 24/7 semi-truck roadside assistance (tire repair, fuel delivery) plus fleet services; Michelin/BFGoodrich commercial dealer. HELD: shop sits on Emma Rd roughly 2+ miles from I-40 Exit 44 via Patton Ave, not at an I-40 interchange, and published phone numbers conflict across sources (828-255-8200 vs 828-348-5093).
held.held_reason: Not located at an I-40 interchange (approx. 2+ miles from Exit 44) and phone conflicts between sources; primary business is auto/fleet tires with truck roadside as secondary service.

excluded.reason: Located off I-26 Exit 44 in Hendersonville per its own website; not at an I-40 exit. No truck wash verified within I-40 exits 37-66.
Commercial truck tire and truck shop located in the Cane Creek/Fletcher industrial area (zip 28732) on the I-26/US 25 corridor, not at an I-40 exit 37-66.

At I-40 Exit 44 but now operates as a standard Speedway gas/convenience station; legacy WilcoHess 'truck stop' listings are outdated and historical directories note no overnight truck parking. Not a current trucker facility.
On US 25/I-26 corridor near Hendersonville, not at an I-40 exit 37-66.

excluded.business_name: I-40 Truck & Trailer Repair / HD Towing
excluded.reason: Shop is in Waynesville (Haywood County), west of the segment boundary (exits 37-66); service model is primarily mobile repair/towing over a 50-mile radius.
Not located at an I-40 interchange (Old Toll Rd, north/east Asheville residential area); operates largely as a mobile/on-call service rather than a fixed roadside facility at an exit.
